hypothesis: Integrating LLM-driven agents with explicit tool access (short-term memory, long-term memory, periodic table, knowledge base), DiffCSP structure generation, and iComFormer property evaluation will yield a higher success rate of discovering inorganic compositions and structures within ±0.25 eV/atom of target formation energies and improved compositional validity, uniqueness, and novelty (V.U.N. score) than tool-free LLMs or diffusion-only baselines, because iterative tool-guided planning, proposition, and feedback enable more informed and constrained candidate generation.
